description: Logic App-style outlines for the six SOAR playbooks. Shaped like workflow definitions (trigger + actions with connector names) so each can be lifted into a real Logic App; kept as outlines because the lab is offline-first. Human-approval boundaries are explicit.

playbooks.purpose: Add identity, asset and PAM context to every new incident so the analyst never starts cold.

actions.operation: Get entities (accounts, IPs)
GET identity inventory (department, title, is_privileged, roles, manager) - Graph API in production, data/identities.json in the lab
GET asset classification for any service principal entities (privilege tier, owner)
GET CyberArk recent checkout history for the account (REST API)
GET threat intelligence verdict for source IPs
Add enrichment as incident comment; apply tags (privileged-identity, vpn-egress, tier-0)

playbooks.rollback: Informational only - nothing to roll back.

playbooks.purpose: Put the right human on the incident inside the acknowledgement SLA.

trigger.when: incident created or severity raised to High/Critical

actions.operation: Build card payload: incident id, title, severity, entities, top 3 triage steps, incident deep link
Post adaptive card to SOC channel
If severity == Critical
POST to PagerDuty/Opsgenie events API (15-minute ack SLA)
Delay 15 minutes, then escalate to secondary on-call if incident unacknowledged

playbooks.rollback: Informational only - mark notification as false alarm.

playbooks.purpose: Guarantee every incident has an auditable work item with SLA timers.

actions.operation: POST incident record: short description, severity-mapped priority, assignment group SOC, link back to Sentinel
Add ticket number as incident tag and comment
On incident close, resolve ticket with disposition (true positive / false positive / posture finding)

playbooks.rollback: Close the ticket with disposition 'opened in error'.

playbooks.purpose: Cut attacker access fast by invalidating refresh tokens and active sessions - cheap to reverse, so it runs unattended at Critical severity.
playbooks.automation_level: full-auto for Critical severity, approval-required otherwise

actions.operation: If severity == Critical continue; else send approval card and wait
Approval card to SOC DRI (skipped at Critical)
Revoke sign-in sessions / invalidate refresh tokens for the account
Confirm user compromised (drives risk-based Conditional Access)
Record action, actor (playbook), timestamp on the incident

playbooks.rollback: None needed - the legitimate user simply re-authenticates with MFA.

playbooks.purpose: Force credential rotation after confirmed credential compromise - always human-approved because resets on service accounts break integrations and mid-attack resets must pair with MFA re-registration.

actions.operation: If target is a service account, stop and route to application owner instead
Approval card to SOC DRI with identity context
Reset password, require change at next sign-in
Require re-register multifactor authentication (remove attacker-enrolled methods)
Out-of-band notification with helpdesk contact
Record action and approval trail

playbooks.rollback: Helpdesk-assisted reset with in-person or video identity verification.

playbooks.purpose: Full account lockout for confirmed takeover - highest blast radius, so it always needs DRI approval and notifies the user's manager.

actions.operation: Pull enrichment from PB-01: is the account privileged, a service account, or on-call staff?
Approval card to SOC DRI including business-impact context
Set accountEnabled = false
Revoke all sessions and refresh tokens
Suspend the user's CyberArk safe access via REST API
Notify the user's manager and the service desk with reinstatement path
Record action, approver, timestamp

playbooks.rollback: Re-enable the account after eviction is confirmed; user re-establishes sessions with fresh credentials and MFA.
